analysis.reasoning: The image displays a multi-line chart showing the annual U.S. energy consumption by source from 1776 to 2023. The chart type is definitively a LineChart. The title 'Annual U.S. energy consumption (1776–2023)' was extracted verbatim. The phrase 'quadrillion British thermal units' clearly describes the y-axis scale and was assigned to `yAxisTitle`, leaving `subtitle` empty to avoid redundancy. The y-axis range was precisely set from `yMin: 0` to `yMax: 45` as depicted. Data points for each energy source (petroleum, natural gas, renewables, coal, nuclear) were meticulously extracted at consistent year intervals, ensuring high numerical fidelity. The original image uses direct labels for series on the right side, not a separate legend, hence `showLegend` is set to `false`. The colors for each series were extracted and adjusted to be vibrant, adhering to the 'Vibrancy Mandate'. Specifically, the black line for 'coal' was replaced with a dark coffee brown (`#5C4033`) to ensure visibility without losing its original dark tone. The annotation 'Coal surpasses wood around 1885.' was included, with `seriesIndex` pointing to 'coal' and `index` to the closest year (1890) for contextual placement. `showValueLabels` is set to `true` to enable the rendering of this annotation.

props.title: Annual U.S. energy consumption (1776–2023)
